rationale: The citing paper is a meta-analysis of personal selling elasticities (506 estimates from 75 papers), with pharmaceuticals as a major context and explicit coding of methodological features; the cited paper provides pharmaceutical brand-level elasticity estimates for detailing/marketing and a specific modeling approach, making it a plausible source study rather than a generic mention. With a direct DOI match (10.1509/jmkr.47.1.103), this pair could reflect substantive use (data point/method/comparison) and should be retained for full relation judgment.
